content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "Across the three client turns, there is no discernible shift. The client opens with a victim-frame question ('why do I always end up with people who just cannot show up?') and by turn 5 is more entrenched in that frame, not less — defending herself ('I was the available one'), listing evidence ('texting first... planning things'), and explicitly relocating blame onto the partners ('I wasn't the one creating the distance. That was them.'). The sharp laugh, eye roll, and 'I knew you were going to say something like that' indicate prickliness/defensiveness intensifying rather than softening. No somatic shift, no new feeling named, no approach to the abyss of being adored, no recognition of her role beyond a sarcastic 'guilty of caring too much.' The breakthrough condition (feeling the avoided receiving-state) was not approached. The conversation is also very short (5 turns), but even within that window the trajectory is hardening, not opening.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening victim frame: 'why do I always end up with people who just cannot show up?' — baseline."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client reframes as 'detective work' but stays at intellectual/curious surface; 'why does this keep happening to me' preserves victim position."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Defensiveness escalates: sharp laugh, eye roll, explicit relocation of blame ('I wasn't the one creating the distance. That was them.'). Hardening, not softening."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic"],
  "notes": "Conversation is only 5 turns, limiting what's possible, but the directional signal in the client is away from the breakthrough condition, not toward it."
}
